support.summary: No CEO action required; early-stage RFP still buyer-controlled
support.detail: Renuity ($300k, Formal Evaluation, win 44) is a fresh multi-vendor RFP that Zycus was only just invited into on 19 Jun; the RFP itself has not been issued yet (slated by 10 Jul). There is no blocked discount, roadmap commitment, presales/POC guarantee, or exec-connect ask on the table, and the economic buyer (COO/ELT) is not yet named or engaged. Every current move (deliver RFP templates, book SC discovery, build the indirect-spend ROI case with champion Alec Diaz) is owner-level field work, not a CEO-irreplaceable action.
support.metric: RFP not yet issued (due 10 Jul); win 44
support.why_not_vp: Nothing here requires the CEO's authority or peer relationship; the economic buyer (COO/ELT) is not yet named or engaged, so no exec-connect is even actionable, and a VP/CRO can drive standard RFP execution.

monitor.reason: Deal is advancing (Qualified->Formal Evaluation on 19 Jun), not slipping; no <=14-day our_slip, slowdown, or competitor-edge signal.
